personal.name: Md Asif Jawed
personal.availability: Available for Senior Backend Engineer Opportunities
personal.description: Senior Backend Engineer with 4+ years of experience building scalable backend systems, payment platforms, distributed architectures, and cloud-native applications using Node.js, TypeScript, PostgreSQL, Redis, and AWS.
about: I am a Senior Backend Engineer with 4+ years of experience designing and operating scalable backend systems.
My expertise includes Node.js, TypeScript, NestJS, PostgreSQL, Redis, AWS, microservices, distributed systems, and payment infrastructure.
I have worked extensively on payment gateway integrations, wallet systems, real-time applications, event-driven architectures, and cloud-native platforms.
Most recently, I designed and launched TarbiyahOS, a SaaS platform built with Next.js, Supabase, PostgreSQL, and modern observability tooling.

experience.description: Designed and launched a parenting-focused SaaS platform while owning product architecture, engineering, deployment, monitoring, analytics, and growth infrastructure.
bullets: Architected and developed the platform using Next.js, TypeScript, Supabase, PostgreSQL, and Tailwind CSS.
Designed authentication flows, role-based access control, onboarding workflows, and database architecture using Supabase Auth and PostgreSQL.
Implemented observability and analytics infrastructure using PostHog, Sentry, and LogRocket.
Established SEO foundations including metadata management, sitemap generation, structured content architecture, and blog workflows.
Managed the complete product lifecycle including development, deployment, monitoring, performance optimization, and production operations.

experience.description: Designed and operated scalable backend services supporting payment systems, wallet infrastructure, and real-time gaming applications.
bullets: Designed and maintained scalable backend services and REST APIs using Node.js, TypeScript, and NestJS.
Architected microservices using event-driven and asynchronous communication patterns with RabbitMQ.
Integrated Cashfree, Razorpay, and Juspay payment gateways with idempotent workflows and retry mechanisms.
Built wallet infrastructure supporting balance tracking, bonus distribution, and transactional consistency.
Implemented real-time communication features using WebSockets and long polling.
Optimized performance using Redis caching strategies and efficient database access patterns.
Worked extensively with AWS services including EKS, Lambda, EC2, S3, SQS, SNS, IAM, CloudWatch, and ElastiCache.
Contributed to CI/CD processes, observability initiatives, technical documentation, and engineering mentorship.

experience.description: Built backend systems and APIs across SaaS, enterprise, and Web3 applications.
bullets: Developed backend services using Node.js, Express.js, and TypeScript.
Built RESTful and GraphQL APIs with a focus on performance and maintainability.
Designed database schemas and optimized queries across PostgreSQL, MySQL, and MongoDB.
Implemented asynchronous processing using queues and event-driven workflows.
Developed backend integrations for Web3 and NFT platforms.
Worked with AWS services including EC2, S3, Lambda, SES, SNS, SQS, and Redis.
Utilized Docker and CI/CD pipelines to streamline deployments.

projects.title: TarbiyahOS
projects.description: A parenting-focused SaaS platform designed to help Muslim families build discipline, routines, character development, and structured family systems.
bullets: Built using Next.js, TypeScript, Supabase, PostgreSQL, and Progressive Web App architecture.
Implemented authentication, onboarding workflows, role-based access control, analytics, and observability tooling.
Integrated PostHog, Sentry, and LogRocket for monitoring, debugging, and product insights.
Established SEO infrastructure including metadata management, sitemap generation, structured content, and blog architecture.

works.title: Building TarbiyahOS: From Idea to Production SaaS
works.intro: TarbiyahOS is a parenting-focused SaaS platform designed to help Muslim families build stronger routines, discipline, and family systems. The project provided an opportunity to own every stage of the product lifecycle from architecture and development to deployment, monitoring, analytics, and SEO.

sections.heading: Why I Built It
sections.content: Many parents struggle with consistency, routines, and long-term character development. TarbiyahOS was created to provide a structured system that combines timeless parenting principles with practical execution.

sections.heading: Architecture Overview
sections.content: The platform is built using Next.js, TypeScript, Supabase, PostgreSQL, and Progressive Web App architecture. The goal was to maximize development velocity while maintaining reliability and scalability.

sections.heading: Why Next.js
sections.content: Next.js provides an excellent developer experience, strong SEO capabilities, server-side rendering, and modern routing features. It allows the marketing site and application experience to coexist within a unified architecture.

sections.heading: Why Supabase
sections.content: Supabase provided authentication, database management, storage, and backend capabilities while keeping the architecture simple and maintainable for a solo product builder.

sections.heading: Authentication Design
sections.content: Authentication is implemented using Supabase Auth with secure session handling, protected routes, onboarding flows, and role-based access controls.

sections.heading: Database Design
sections.content: PostgreSQL was selected for its reliability, relational modeling capabilities, and long-term maintainability. The schema was designed to support structured content, user onboarding, and future product growth.

sections.heading: Analytics & Observability
sections.content: PostHog is used for product analytics and user behavior insights. Sentry and LogRocket provide error monitoring, debugging, and production visibility.

sections.heading: SEO Strategy
sections.content: The marketing site is optimized through metadata management, sitemap generation, structured content, and blog architecture to support long-term organic growth.

sections.heading: Challenges & Lessons Learned
sections.content: Building TarbiyahOS reinforced the importance of balancing product development, architecture decisions, observability, SEO, and deployment processes. The project strengthened my understanding of full product ownership while maintaining engineering quality.
